name: Verify resume claims against GitHub and LinkedIn and score candidate authenticity

parameters.content:
## Verify resume claims against GitHub and LinkedIn and score candidate authenticity

This workflow automatically validates candidate resume claims by cross-referencing public data sources, portfolios, and skill benchmarks — then generates a structured authenticity score report.

### Who's it for
• Recruiters screening high-volume applicants
• HR teams conducting pre-interview verification
• Hiring managers validating technical skill claims
• Staffing agencies performing candidate due diligence

### How it works / What it does
1. Accepts resume data via webhook or scheduled batch
2. Parses and extracts all verifiable claims
3. Cross-references GitHub, LinkedIn, education & employment sources
4. Benchmarks technical skills against known standards
5. Calculates weighted authenticity scores per claim category
6. Generates discrepancy report and confidence levels
7. Logs full verification report to Google Sheets

### How to set up
1. Import this workflow
2. Set up credentials (GitHub API, LinkedIn API, OpenAI/Anthropic, Google Sheets)
3. Configure scoring weights and threshold values in the Set node
4. Activate workflow

### Requirements
• Webhook endpoint or scheduled trigger
• GitHub API token
• LinkedIn API access (or RapidAPI LinkedIn proxy)
• Google Sheets (for output logging)
• OpenAI / Anthropic API

### How to customize the workflow
• Adjust scoring weights in the Set Scoring Config node
• Add/remove verification sources in the HTTP Request nodes
• Modify Python claim extractor keywords
• Update Google Sheet columns to match your ATS schema

parameters.pythonCode:
# ============================================================
# EXTRACT & PARSE VERIFIABLE CLAIMS FROM RESUME TEXT
# ============================================================

item = _input.item.json

resume = (item.get('resumeText', '') or '').strip()

import re

# ============================================================
# 1. EMPLOYMENT HISTORY EXTRACTION
# ============================================================

employment_patterns = [
    r'(?P<title>[A-Z][\w\s]+?(?:Engineer|Developer|Manager|Analyst|Architect|Lead|Director|Specialist|Consultant|Designer|Scientist)[\w\s]*?)\s*(?:at|@|,|-)\s*(?P<company>[A-Z][\w\s&.,]+?)\s*(?:from|\()?\s*(?P<start>(?:Jan(?:uary)?|Feb(?:ruary)?|Mar(?:ch)?|Apr(?:il)?|May|Jun(?:e)?|Jul(?:y)?|Aug(?:ust)?|Sep(?:tember)?|Oct(?:ober)?|Nov(?:ember)?|Dec(?:ember)?|\d{4})[\w\s,]*)\s*(?:–|-|to|—)\s*(?P<end>(?:Present|Current|Now|Jan(?:uary)?|Feb(?:ruary)?|Mar(?:ch)?|Apr(?:il)?|May|Jun(?:e)?|Jul(?:y)?|Aug(?:ust)?|Sep(?:tember)?|Oct(?:ober)?|Nov(?:ember)?|Dec(?:ember)?|\d{4})[\w\s,]*)',
]

employment_claims = []
seen_employment = set()

for pat in employment_patterns:
    for m in re.finditer(pat, resume, re.IGNORECASE):
        title   = (m.group('title')   or '').strip()
        company = (m.group('company') or '').strip()
        start   = (m.group('start')   or '').strip()
        end     = (m.group('end')     or '').strip()

        dedup_key = f"{title.lower()}|{company.lower()}"
        if dedup_key in seen_employment:
            continue
        seen_employment.add(dedup_key)

        employment_claims.append({
            'type':    'employment',
            'title':   title,
            'company': company,
            'start':   start,
            'end':     end
        })

# ============================================================
# 2. EDUCATION EXTRACTION
# ============================================================

edu_pattern = r'(?P<degree>Bachelor(?:\'?s)?|Master(?:\'?s)?|PhD|Ph\.D|B\.?S\.?|M\.?S\.?|MBA|B\.?E\.?|M\.?E\.?|Associate(?:\'?s)?|Doctorate)[\w\s.]*?(?:in|of)?\s*(?P<field>[\w\s]+?)\s*(?:from|at|,|-)\s*(?P<institution>[A-Z][\w\s]+?(?:University|College|Institute|School|Academy)[\w\s]*?)(?=\n|,|\.|$)'

edu_claims = []
seen_edu = set()

for m in re.finditer(edu_pattern, resume, re.IGNORECASE):
    degree      = (m.group('degree')      or '').strip()
    field       = (m.group('field')       or '').strip()
    institution = (m.group('institution') or '').strip()

    dedup_key = f"{degree.lower()}|{institution.lower()}"
    if dedup_key in seen_edu:
        continue
    seen_edu.add(dedup_key)

    edu_claims.append({
        'type':        'education',
        'degree':      degree,
        'field':       field,
        'institution': institution
    })

# ============================================================
# 3. SKILLS EXTRACTION
# ============================================================

skill_section_pattern = r'(?:Technical\s+)?(?:Skills?|Technologies?|Tech\s+Stack|Tools?|Expertise|Proficiencies?|Competencies?)[:\s\n]+([^\n#]{10,300})'

skill_list  = []
seen_skills = set()

for section_match in re.finditer(skill_section_pattern, resume, re.IGNORECASE):
    raw_section = section_match.group(1) or ''
    raw_skills  = re.split(r'[,\n|•·/]', raw_section)

    for s in raw_skills:
        cleaned = s.strip().strip('*-–').strip()
        if 2 < len(cleaned) < 40 and cleaned.lower() not in seen_skills:
            seen_skills.add(cleaned.lower())
            skill_list.append(cleaned)

        if len(skill_list) >= 30:
            break

skill_claims = [{'type': 'skill', 'name': s} for s in skill_list]

# ============================================================
# 4. CERTIFICATION EXTRACTION
# ============================================================

cert_patterns = [
    r'(?:Certified|Certificate|Certification\s+in|Certified\s+in)[:\s]+([A-Z][\w\s]+?(?:AWS|GCP|Azure|PMP|CPA|CFA|CISSP|CompTIA|Scrum|Google|Microsoft|Oracle|Salesforce|PMI|ITIL|CEH|CISM|CISA)[\w\s]*?)(?=\n|,|\.|$)',
    r'(?:AWS|GCP|Azure|Google|Microsoft|Oracle|Salesforce)\s+(?:Certified\s+)?[\w\s]+?(?:Associate|Professional|Expert|Developer|Architect|Engineer|Practitioner)(?=\n|,|\.|$)',
]

cert_claims  = []
seen_certs   = set()

for pat in cert_patterns:
    for m in re.finditer(pat, resume, re.IGNORECASE):
        cert_name = (m.group(1) if m.lastindex and m.lastindex >= 1 else m.group(0)).strip()
        if cert_name.lower() not in seen_certs:
            seen_certs.add(cert_name.lower())
            cert_claims.append({'type': 'certification', 'name': cert_name})

# ============================================================
# 5. YEAR RANGE SANITY CHECK
# ============================================================

year_pattern  = r'\b(19[8-9]\d|20[0-2]\d)\b'
years_found   = [int(y) for y in re.findall(year_pattern, resume)]
timeline_flag = False
timeline_note = ''

if len(years_found) >= 2:
    span = max(years_found) - min(years_found)
    if span > 40:
        timeline_flag = True
        timeline_note = f'Suspicious year span detected: {min(years_found)} to {max(years_found)}'

# ============================================================
# 6. ASSEMBLE OUTPUT
# ============================================================

all_claims = employment_claims + edu_claims + skill_claims + cert_claims

return {
    'json': {
        **item,
        'parsedClaims': all_claims,
        'claimCounts': {
            'employment':     len(employment_claims),
            'education':      len(edu_claims),
            'skills':         len(skill_claims),
            'certifications': len(cert_claims),
            'total':          len(all_claims)
        },
        'timelineFlag': timeline_flag,
        'timelineNote': timeline_note,
        'resumeLength': len(resume)
    }
}
